1.CLI Commands2.Configuration2.1Frontmatter2.2Layouts2.3Themes3.Directives

Layout

Layouts behave the same way as themes but have a different purpose. Themes define the global theming of elements in your website, whereas layouts are responsible for laying out what shows up automatically and where it should go. This page forcibly uses the topnav layout, whereas the rest of this website uses the sidebar layout.

topnav

The topnav layout (used on this page) places the navigation bar at the top of the page. The navigation bar contains the website's title on the left and the navigation links on the right. The content of the page goes below it. Sub-pages are available when hovering the main buttons.

sidebar

The sidebar layout (used on the rest of the site) places the navigation to the left side of the page. The navigation bar displays The title on the top, and the rest of the navigations links below it, with numbered sections, subsections and subsubsections. Some divider text also renders to help organize furthermore sections. The content goes to the right of the sidebar.

summary.mq used for these examples

summary.mq
[Home](index.mq)

User Guide # only in sidebar

[Docs](docs.mq)
For developers

In the Marque repository, the layouts are defined as .css files inside of template/layouts/ and copied into each new project at marque new <name>. They directly apply onto the index.html inside of template/themes/ when building the project. When creating new layouts, make sure you match the classes used in the index.html file.

layout
template/
├── layouts/
│   ├── sidebar.css
│   └── topnav.css
│
└── themes/
	├── index.html
	├── index.js
	└── *.css
Previous↩ FrontmatterNextThemes ↪
Built with Marque
